1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Project Progress
2	
3	## Current Stage
4	
5	prover (iteration 078 — **three substantive prover lanes**, all targeting real sorry-count reduction).
6	
7	## Stages
8	- [x] init
9	- [x] autoformalize
10	- [ ] prover
11	- [ ] polish
12	
13	## Big-picture context
14	
15	This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.
16	
17	`STRATEGY.md` describes the path: Phase A (cohomology) + Phase B (differentials) + Phase C (Picard scheme, multi-step gap-fill) + Phase D (genus / Jacobian / instances, closed-by-reduction since iter-073) + Phase E (Abel–Jacobi, closed iter-073).
18	
19	Iter-077 was the first substantive-progress iteration since iter-073 (net 19 → 17 sorries; -2). Highlights:
20	- `cotangentExactSeqBeta` fully closed.
21	- `f_R.map_smul'` (in `BasicOpenCech.lean`) closed via literal `e_i.toAddEquiv.module R` instance (`letI` + `exact`, not `have` + `convert`).
22	- `Jacobian.lean` compilation repaired (6 latent errors → 0); cascade fix for `AbelJacobi.lean`.
23	- `Modules/Monoidal.lean` scaffolded by the refactor subagent (Phase C step C0 Lean-side begun; bodies = `sorry`).
24	- `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` partially closed (4 of 5 fields done; `d_app` retains 1 internal sorry).
25	
26	Current project state (independently verified this plan pass via `sorry_analyzer.py` + `lean_diagnostic_messages`):
27	
28	| File | Sorries | Compiles? | Phase | Iter-078 plan |
29	|---|---|---|---|---|
30	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` | 6 | yes | A | **Lane 2: close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (S1–S8 recipe)** |
31	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` | 6 | yes | B | **Lane 1: close `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app` (ζ-bridge)** |
32	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` | 3 | yes | C0 | **Lane 3: fill all three bodies** |
33	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean` | 1 | yes | D done | off-limits (`nonempty_jacobianWitness` deferred to iter-085+) |
34	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/Functor.lean` | 1 | yes | C-rep | off-limits |
35	| `AlgebraicJacobian/AbelJacobi.lean` | 0 | yes | E done | off-limits |
36	| `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean` | 0 | yes | D done | off-limits |
37	| **Total syntactic** | **17** | — | | |
38	
39	## Current Objectives
40	
41	The dispatcher fans out one prover per file listed below. Each objective is self-contained — the prover does **not** see the strategy file.
42	
43	### 1. `AlgebraicJacobian/Differentials.lean` — close `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app`
44	
45	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L122 `relativeDifferentialsPresheaf_isSheaf`, **L260 `cotangentExactSeqAlpha.d_app`** (internal sorry — this lane's target), L387 `cotangentExactSeq_structure`, L671 `smooth_iff_locally_free_omega`, L688 `cotangent_at_section`, L830 `serre_duality_genus`.
46	
47	**Goal.** Close the single internal sorry inside `cotangentExactSeqAlpha`'s `d_app` field at L260. Preserve the four other fields of the `d_target` struct (already closed iter-077: `d`, `d_mul`, `d_map`, and the `d_smul` implicit).
48	
49	**Mathematical content.** The `d_app` field must show `D_X.d ((f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)) = 0`, where `D_X = derivation' φ_fg'` is the universal derivation pushed forward along `f`, and `φ_g'`, `φ_fg'` are the canonical morphisms encoding the universal derivations on $\Omega_{Y/S}$ and $\Omega_{X/S}$ respectively.
50	
51	The mathematical fact: every element in the image of `f.c.app U ∘ φ_g'.app U` is in the kernel of `D_X.d` because it factors through the universal-derivation structure on $\Omega_{X/S}$. Concretely, `(f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)` is a section that factors through `(φ_fg'.app (f⁻¹U)).hom` of some lift `(ζ.app U) a`, and the universal-derivation axiom says `Derivation'.d` annihilates anything pulled back through `φ_fg'`.
52	
53	The technical blocker: the identity
54	$$
55	  \varphi_{g'} \;\circ\; f.c \;=\; \mathrm{adj}_f.\mathrm{unit} \;\circ\; (\mathrm{pushforward}\,f).\mathrm{map}\,\varphi_{fg'}
56	$$
57	is no longer `rfl` against current Mathlib: it asserts the pullback composition equality `pullback f ∘ pullback g = pullback (f≫g)`, which holds up to iso (by uniqueness of left adjoints — both sides are left adjoint to `pushforward (f≫g) = pushforward g ∘ pushforward f`) but is not definitional, since `pullback := (Opens.map _).op.lan` is built via the left Kan extension's universal property.
58	
59	**Recipe.** Build a `ζ`-bridge analogous to the η-bridge that worked for `cotangentExactSeqBeta` (iter-077). Two equivalent routes:
60	
61	1. **ζ-bridge construction** (recommended — mirrors iter-077 beta success). Define
62	   ```
63	   ζ : pullback g S.presheaf ⟶ (pushforward f).obj (pullback (f≫g) S.presheaf)
64	   ```
65	   via `adj_fg.homEquiv.symm` of a specific morphism `pullback g S.presheaf ⟶ pullback (f≫g) S.presheaf` (dual to the η in beta). Then pointwise:
66	   ```
67	   (f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a) = (φ_fg'.app (f⁻¹U)).hom ((ζ.app U) a)
68	   ```
69	   This puts the LHS in the image of `φ_fg'.app`, and `Derivation'.d_app _ _` closes the goal.
70	
71	2. **Pullback-composition iso** (alternative). Search Mathlib for a natural iso `pullback f ∘ pullback g ≅ pullback (f≫g)` of `TopCat.Presheaf` functors. Candidate names to try via `lean_leansearch` / `lean_loogle`: `lan_comp`, `lanCompIso`, `Functor.compLeftAdjoint`, `Adjunction.leftAdjointUniq`. If found, use it to rewrite `(f.c.app U).hom (φ_g'.app U a)` into the form `(φ_fg'.app …).hom (...)`.
72	
73	**The prover should attempt route (1) first.** The iter-077 task report explicitly recommends it as the analogue of the beta closure. If route (1) hits a wall, switch to route (2) only after exhausting the homEquiv-coherence lemmas (`Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_right`, `Adjunction.homEquiv_naturality_left`, `Equiv.apply_symm_apply`).
74	
75	**Heartbeat budget.** The iter-077 prover required `set_option maxHeartbeats 16000000 in` on the `cotangentExactSeqAlpha` def. **Keep this option in place** — do not remove it.
76	
77	**Hard constraints**:
78	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11).
79	- **No new axioms.**
80	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation** of candidate bodies (user policy). `lean_diagnostic_messages` after each save IS allowed (standard dev feedback loop).
81	- Preserve the four iter-077-closed `d_target` fields (`d`, `d_mul`, `d_map`, smul) byte-for-byte unless the d_app fix forces an intentional rewrite.
82	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `d_app`); hard cap **6** (no regression).
83	
84	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Differentials.tex` § "Cotangent exact sequence" (`thm:cotangentExactSeqAlpha`).
85	
86	### 2. `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean` — close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`
87	
88	**File status.** 6 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Sorries: L495, L819, L847 (3 dead-end substeps — stay), **L1079 `h_diff_pi_smul_f`** (this lane's target), L1119 `g_R.map_smul'`, L1148 `h_loc_exact`.
89	
90	**Goal.** Close `h_diff_pi_smul_f` (L1079), the f-side per-summand R-linearity decomposition through the 5-layer functor stack. This is substantive content — closing it reduces the sorry count by 1 (no sorry-trade).
91	
92	**Mathematical content.** The statement `h_diff_pi_smul_f` packages: for `r : R` and `y : ∀ i, Z₁ i` (where `Z₁ i = X.basicOpenCover_inter f i`, the affine basic open at index `i`), the f-side cochain differential `scK₀.f` applied to the smul `r • y` equals `r • (scK₀.f y)`, both computed via the canonical `e_i : ↑(∏ᶜ Z_i) ≃ scK₀.X_i` linear equivalences.
93	
94	The proof requires unfolding the 5-layer functor stack and chasing the R-linearity through each layer. The decomposition (documented in-place at L1009–L1067) is:
95	
96	- **S1: `funext j`** to get the goal componentwise in the codomain index `j`. (NOT in indexed-sum form — this was the iter-075 mistake; the goal post-`funext` is `(scK₀.f) (r • y) j = r • (scK₀.f y) j`.)
97	- **S2: `dsimp only [scK₀, K₀, cechCochain, cechComplexFunctor, toModuleKSheaf, toModuleKPresheaf_obj]`** unfolds the 5-layer functor stack so that `scK₀.f` is exposed as the alternating-sum cochain differential.
98	- **S3:** Expose the differential as `∑ k, (-1)^k * (restriction along δ_k)`. Use `dsimp` or explicit unfold of `HomologicalComplex.d` + `CochainComplex.up`.
99	- **S4: `Pi.smul_apply`** evaluates `(r • y) j` to `r • (y j)`.
100	- **S5: `Finset.smul_sum`** distributes `r •` across the alternating sum.
101	- **S6: `Finset.sum_congr rfl fun k _ => ?_`** reduces to per-summand R-linearity.
102	- **S7:** Per-summand `r • (restriction along δ_k applied to y_{j∘δ_k}) = restriction along δ_k applied to (r • y_{j∘δ_k})`. Decompose via: (i) `Pi.smul_apply` on LHS at output index `j`, (ii) `RingHom.map_mul` distributes the restriction across `r * y_{j∘δ_k}`, (iii) `algebraMap_naturality` says the restriction respects the algebraMap (the R-action factors through the structure-sheaf restriction map), (iv) reverse `Pi.smul_apply` for `h_mod_pi₂` at output index `j`.
103	- **S8: `rfl` or `ring`** closes after the chain.
104	
105	**Why iter-075/076 failed.** Iter-075 added the chain with `try`-wrappers; iter-076 dropped the wrappers and surfaced an error: the `simp` chain didn't fire because the goal post-`funext` is componentwise, not in indexed-sum form. The S1–S8 recipe specifically addresses this by unfolding *before* attempting the sum manipulations.
106	
107	**Hard constraints**:
108	- **No new project-local helper lemmas** (user policy 2026-05-11).
109	- **No new axioms.**
110	- **No `lean_run_code` pre-validation.**
111	- Sorry-count budget: starts at 6; target **≤ 5** (close `h_diff_pi_smul_f`); hard cap **6** (no regression).
112	- The L495 / L819 / L847 sorries are dead-ends — out of scope for this lane.
113	- `g_R.map_smul'` (L1119) and `h_loc_exact` (L1148) are downstream — out of scope this iter (they need infrastructure that's gated on this lane).
114	
115	**Blueprint**: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § "Čech acyclicity for the structure sheaf on affine basic-open covers". The per-summand R-linearity is internal to `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` and not a top-level declaration with its own blueprint label.
116	
117	### 3. `AlgebraicJacobian/Modules/Monoidal.lean` — fill the three Phase-C-C0 bodies
118	
119	**File status.** 3 syntactic sorries; file compiles cleanly (0 errors). Scaffolded by the iter-077 refactor subagent. Sorries: L55 `tensorObj`, L62 `instMonoidalCategoryStruct`, L67 `instMonoidalCategory`.
120	
121	**Goal.** Fill all three sorries so that the file declares a complete `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` instance for every scheme `X`. This is the Phase C step C0 closure — unblocks the iter-079+ refinement of `LineBundle` to invertible $\mathcal O_X$-modules.
122	
123	**Mathematical content** (see blueprint `Modules_Monoidal.tex`, theorem `thm:Modules_MonoidalCategory`):
124	
125	For two $\mathcal O_X$-modules $M, N$, the tensor product is
126	$$
127	  M \otimes_{\mathcal O_X} N \;:=\; \mathrm{sheafify}\bigl(M_{\mathrm{psh}} \otimes_{\mathcal O_X, \mathrm{psh}} N_{\mathrm{psh}}\bigr),
128	$$
129	where $(-)_{\mathrm{psh}}$ is the forgetful functor from $\mathrm{SheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$ to $\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}\,\mathcal O_X$, and the presheaf tensor product is the pointwise one supplied by Mathlib's `PresheafOfModules.monoidalCategory`. The tensor unit is the structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$ regarded as the trivial $\mathcal O_X$-module. The associator, unitors, and braiding are sheafifications of their presheaf counterparts; the pentagon/triangle/hexagon coherences transfer via the universal property of sheafification (sheafification is a left adjoint to inclusion, so commutes with colimits, and tensor is a colimit-preserving bifunctor).
130	
131	**Recipe.**
132	
133	**(a) `tensorObj` (L55).** The body should construct `X.Modules` from the sheafification of the presheaf tensor product. The shortest path uses three Mathlib pieces:
134	- `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj` — the pointwise presheaf tensor product.
135	- `PresheafOfModules.sheafification` — the sheafification functor (left adjoint to the forgetful `SheafOfModules → PresheafOfModules`).
136	- `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` — the round-trip natural iso `(sheafify ∘ forgetful) ≅ id` (in one direction).
137	
138	Outline:
139	```
140	noncomputable def tensorObj (M N : X.Modules) : X.Modules :=
141	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafification _).obj
142	    (PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj M.val N.val)
143	```
144	(Exact name resolution may require chasing through `SheafOfModules.toSheafCompSheafToPresheafIso` or a similar API. The prover should `lean_local_search` / `lean_leansearch` to find the correct constructor.)
145	
146	**(b) `instMonoidalCategoryStruct` (L62).** Combine `tensorObj` with the tensor unit (structure sheaf $\mathcal O_X$), and the associator/unitors/braiding inherited via sheafification. Outline:
147	```
148	noncomputable instance instMonoidalCategoryStruct :
149	    MonoidalCategoryStruct (X.Modules) where
150	  tensorObj := tensorObj X
151	  tensorUnit := …  -- the structure sheaf as a SheafOfModules
152	  whiskerLeft := …
153	  whiskerRight := …
154	  tensorHom := …
155	  associator := …
156	  leftUnitor := …
157	  rightUnitor := …
158	```
159	The whisker/tensorHom/associator/unitors all transfer from the presheaf side by sheafifying the presheaf-side morphism. The prover may find it easier to provide one comprehensive `where`-block than to delegate via `inferInstance` chains.
160	
161	**(c) `instMonoidalCategory` (L67).** Provides the pentagon, triangle, and hexagon coherence axioms. Each transfers from the presheaf side via the universal property of sheafification; if `Sheaf.monoidalCategory` (Mathlib's generic instance) is applicable, defer to it; otherwise build the four `*.naturality`/coherence proofs explicitly using `SheafOfModules.hom_ext` to descend to the presheaf-side check.
162	
163	**Mathlib precedent.** The generic pattern is `CategoryTheory.Sheaf.monoidalCategory` (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sheaf/Monoidal.lean`), which lifts a symmetric monoidal structure from a value category $A$ to $\mathrm{Sheaf}\,J\,A$ for fixed $A$. Our wrinkle: the value category varies with the open set (we have a sheaf of rings, not a fixed ring). The blueprint's "Mathlib precedent" remark notes this gap. The prover should:

165	2. **Otherwise**, build the structure explicitly using the presheaf-side data + sheafification universal property.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Search Protocol
66	
67	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
68	
69	1. `lean_local_search` first
70	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
71	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
72	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
73	
74	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
75	
76	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
77	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
78	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
79	
80	## Logging
81	
82	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).

85	
86	```markdown
87	# Algebra/WLocal.lean

104	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective
105	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
106	```
107	
108	**Rules:**
109	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
110	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
111	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
112	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
113	
114	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
115	
116	## Summary Pipeline
117	
118	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
119	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
120	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
121	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
122	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
123	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
124	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
125	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):

135	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietorisCover
7	
8	/-!
9	# Basic-open cover wrappers and the substantive Čech acyclicity theorem
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 3) contains the `basicOpenCover` family
12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the
14	`cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt` lemma, and the
15	substantive theorem `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
16	(iter-056 → iter-062). The latter currently carries two labelled substep
17	sorries plus the iter-062 `h_a_fun` scaffolding.
18	
19	This is cohort 3 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the
24	  substantive Čech acyclicity theorem.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98

100	Čech-acyclic cover.

150	
151	Given a Set `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` and indices `i j : s`, the intersection of the
152	two basic-open cover members `basicOpenCover s i` and `basicOpenCover s j`
153	equals the basic open of the product `i.1 * j.1`. Direct iteration of Mathlib's
154	`Scheme.basicOpen_mul`.

156	This is the foundational identification for iter-057+ Čech complex evaluation:
157	the Čech cochain at degree 1 indexes pairs `(i, j) : s × s` and assigns the
158	sheaf evaluated at `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j`. Via this lemma,
159	that intersection is identified with `C.left.basicOpen (i.1 * j.1)`, which by
160	`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the localization
161	`Localization.Away (i.1 * j.1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. The Koszul-style
162	alternating-sum exactness on these localizations follows from

168	    basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓ basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j =
169	      C.left.basicOpen ((i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) * j.1) := by
170	  unfold basicOpenCover
171	  exact (Scheme.basicOpen_mul C.left _ _).symm

185	step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument (Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5

188	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
189	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
190	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
191	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓
192	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j) := by
193	  unfold basicOpenCover

197	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection identification (Finset.inf' form).
198	
199	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
200	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` equals the basic open of the
201	finite product `∏ i ∈ t, i.1` in `Γ(C.left, U)`. Generalises iter-056's binary
202	`basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing.
203	
204	Body via `Finset.cons_induction`: the empty case is impossible by `t.Nonempty`
205	(closed via `Finset.not_nonempty_empty`); the cons case splits on whether the
206	residual `t` is empty or non-empty (via `Finset.eq_empty_or_nonempty`). In the
207	empty residual case, `simp [basicOpenCover]` closes via single-element reduction
208	of `Finset.inf'` and `Finset.prod`. In the non-empty residual case, decompose
209	via `Finset.inf'_cons hne` to expose `basicOpenCover s a ⊓ rest`, decompose
210	`Finset.prod_cons` to expose `a.1 * (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, apply the IH to identify
211	`rest = C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, then `change ... ⊓ _ = _` followed by
212	`rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]` consolidates the product form.
213	
214	This is the foundational identification for iter-058+ Čech complex evaluation
215	at arbitrary degree `n`: the Čech cochain at degree `n` indexes `x : Fin (n+1) → s`
216	and assigns the sheaf evaluated at `⨅ i : Fin (n+1), basicOpenCover s (x i)`.
217	Via this lemma (instantiated at `t := (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))).image x`,
218	or more directly via `Finset.univ.inf' Finset.univ_nonempty (basicOpenCover s ∘ x)`),
219	that intersection identifies with `C.left.basicOpen (∏ i : Fin (n+1), (x i).1)`,
220	which by `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the
221	localization `Localization.Away (∏ i, (x i).1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. -/

223	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
224	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
225	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
226	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) =
227	      C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U))) := by
228	  classical
229	  induction t using Finset.cons_induction with
230	  | empty => exact absurd h Finset.not_nonempty_empty
231	  | cons a t ha ih =>
232	    rcases t.eq_empty_or_nonempty with rfl | hne
233	    · simp [basicOpenCover]
234	    · rw [Finset.inf'_cons hne, Finset.prod_cons, ih hne]
235	      change C.left.basicOpen (a.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) ⊓ _ = _
236	      rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]

283	```

293	(basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod ...)` to land

320	Threading the algebra structure as part of the conclusion type — matching Mathlib's

324	basic-open identification side. The iter-060+ basic-open Koszul acyclicity argument
325	(per the iter-059 analogy report at `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`, Branch A
326	substep 2) chains iter-057's equality + iter-058's inclusion + iter-059's localization
327	+ `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean`)

330	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization
331	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
332	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
333	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
334	    @IsLocalization.Away _ _ (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)))
335	      Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) _
336	      ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE
337	        (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h)).op).hom.toAlgebra) :=
338	  hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen _

342	/-- Iter-066 helper: in `ModuleCat k`, a product projection induced by an
343	injection of index sets is a split epi.
344	
345	Given an injection `f : β → α` and a family `M : α → ModuleCat k`, the natural
346	projection `∏ᶜ_a M a ⟶ ∏ᶜ_b M (f b)` defined as `Pi.lift (fun b => Pi.π M (f b))`
347	(which sends a tuple indexed by `α` to its `image f` components, reindexed by `β`)
348	admits a section in `ModuleCat k`. The section is the "extend by zero" map:
349	given a tuple indexed by `β`, fill in the components in `image f` (using the
350	unique preimage given by injectivity of `f`) and set the remaining components
351	to `0`.
352	
353	This is the categorical fact underlying `h_π_split` in
354	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`: the cochain-complex map
355	`π : K ⟶ K₀` is at each degree of this form (the `↑s₀ ↪ s` inclusion induces a
356	postcomposition map `(Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀) → (Fin (i+1) → s)` which is injective by
357	injectivity of the inclusion, hence the induced product projection on cochain
358	factors is split).
359	
360	The helper is stated for a generic injection so it can be reused for both the
361	`s` substep (`h_π_split` proper) and the slice-cover variants. -/
362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec

393	/-- Iter-060 helper: bridge from `ExactAt n` on the Čech cochain complex to
394	`Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)`.
395	
396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an
397	`ExactAt n` hypothesis into `IsZero ((cechCochain ...).homology n)`, and
398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`
399	object to `Subsingleton` on its underlying carrier type.
400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
413	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
414	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
415	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
416	schemes in our setting.
417	
418	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
419	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
420	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
421	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
422	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
423	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
424	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
425	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
426	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
428	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
429	
430	The structural skeleton is:
431	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
432	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
433	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
434	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
435	
436	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
437	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
438	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
439	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
440	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
441	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
442	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
443	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
444	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
445	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
446	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
447	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
448	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
449	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
450	
451	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
452	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
453	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
454	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
455	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
456	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
457	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
458	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
459	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by

161:lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
187:        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality

140	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
141	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
142	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
143	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
144	
145	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
146	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
147	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
148	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
149	  rfl
150	
151	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
152	natural in `U`. -/
153	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
154	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
155	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
156	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
157	  rfl
158	
159	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
160	`algebraMap`. -/
161	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
162	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
163	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
164	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
165	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
166	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)
167	    (kToSection_naturality (C := C) f)
168	
169	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf
170	
171	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
172	
173	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
174	
175	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (6): the presheaf of `k`-modules built from
176	`O_C` and the structure-morphism algebra structure. -/
177	noncomputable def toModuleKPresheaf (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))) :
178	    (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k where
179	  obj U := ModuleCat.of k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)
180	  map {U V} f := ModuleCat.ofHom
181	    { toFun := fun x => (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom x
182	      map_add' := fun x y => by
183	        simp [(C.left.presheaf.map f).hom.map_add]
184	      map_smul' := fun r x => by
185	        simp only [Algebra.smul_def, RingHom.map_mul, RingHom.id_apply]
186	        congr 1
187	        exact AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality
188	          (C := C) f r }
189	  map_id U := by
190	    ext x
191	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
192	      ModuleCat.hom_id, LinearMap.id_coe, id_eq]
193	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id U)) x
194	  map_comp {U V W} f g := by
195	    ext x
196	    simp only [ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, LinearMap.coe_mk, AddHom.coe_mk,
197	      ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
198	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_comp f g)) x

184	in `FormalCoproduct C` which sends `⦋n⦌` to `U.power (Fin (n + 1))`. -/

186	noncomputable def cech (U : FormalCoproduct.{w} C) :

1	/-

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingFaceMapComplex
9	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproducts.Cech
10	
11	/-!
12	# Cech cohomology
13	
14	Given a family of objects `U : ι → C` in a category `C` that has finite products,
15	we define a Cech complex functor
16	`cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends a presheaf
17	`P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` in a preadditive category (where products exist) to the cochain
18	complex which in degree `n` consists of the product, indexed by `i : Fin (n + 1) → ι`,
19	of the value of `P` on the product of the objects `U (i a)` for `a : Fin (n + 1)`.
20	
21	-/
22	
23	@[expose] public section
24	
25	universe w t v v' u u'
26	
27	namespace CategoryTheory
28	
29	open Limits
30	
31	variable {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {A : Type u'} [Category.{v'} A] [HasProducts.{w} A]
32	
33	namespace Limits.FormalCoproduct
34	
35	open Opposite
36	
37	variable (E : SimplicialObject (FormalCoproduct.{w} C))
38	
39	/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
40	functor `(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CosimplicialObject A` which sends `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
41	cosimplicial object which sends `⦋n⦌` to the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _⦋n⦌`. -/
42	@[simps!]
43	noncomputable def cosimplicialObjectFunctor :
44	    (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CosimplicialObject A :=
45	  evalOp.{w} C A ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringLeft _ _ _).obj E.rightOp
46	
47	variable [Preadditive A]
48	
49	/-- Given a simplicial object `E` in the category `FormalCoproduct C`, this is the
50	functor `(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
51	cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the "evaluation" of `P` on `E _⦋n⦌`. -/
52	@[simps!]
53	noncomputable def cochainComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
54	  cosimplicialObjectFunctor E ⋙ AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex A
55	
56	end Limits.FormalCoproduct
57	
58	variable [HasFiniteProducts C] [Preadditive A] {ι : Type w} (U : ι → C)
59	
60	/-- Given a family of objects `U : ι → C`, this is the Cech complex functor
61	`(Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ` which sends a presheaf `P : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A` to the
62	cochain complex which in degree `n` consists of the product,
63	indexed by `x : Fin (n + 1) → ι`, of the value of `P` on the product of the
64	objects `U (x i)` for `i : Fin (n + 1)`. -/
65	noncomputable def cechComplexFunctor : (Cᵒᵖ ⥤ A) ⥤ CochainComplex A ℕ :=
66	  FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor (FormalCoproduct.mk _ U).cech
67	
68	end CategoryTheory

285	variable (A)
286	
287	/-- The inclusion map of the Moore complex in the alternating face map complex,
288	as a natural transformation -/
289	@[simps]
290	def inclusionOfMooreComplex : normalizedMooreComplex A ⟶ alternatingFaceMapComplex A where
291	  app := inclusionOfMooreComplexMap
292	
293	namespace AlternatingCofaceMapComplex
294	
295	variable (X Y : CosimplicialObject C)
296	
297	/-- The differential on the alternating coface map complex is the alternate
298	sum of the coface maps -/
299	@[simp]
300	def objD (n : ℕ) : X.obj ⦋n⦌ ⟶ X.obj ⦋n + 1⦌ :=
301	  ∑ i : Fin (n + 2), (-1 : ℤ) ^ (i : ℕ) • X.δ i
302	
303	theorem d_eq_unop_d (n : ℕ) :
304	    objD X n =
305	      (AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD ((cosimplicialSimplicialEquiv C).functor.obj (op X))
306	          n).unop := by
307	  simp only [objD, AlternatingFaceMapComplex.objD, unop_sum, unop_zsmul]
308	  rfl
309	
310	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
311	theorem d_squared (n : ℕ) : objD X n ≫ objD X (n + 1) = 0 := by
312	  simp only [d_eq_unop_d, ← unop_comp, AlternatingFaceMapComplex.d_squared, unop_zero]
313	
314	/-- The alternating coface map complex, on objects -/
315	def obj : CochainComplex C ℕ :=
316	  CochainComplex.of (fun n => X.obj ⦋n⦌) (objD X) (d_squared X)
317	
318	variable {X} {Y}
319	
320	/-- The alternating face map complex, on morphisms -/
321	@[simp]
322	def map (f : X ⟶ Y) : obj X ⟶ obj Y :=
323	  CochainComplex.ofHom _ _ _ _ _ _ (fun n => f.app ⦋n⦌) fun n => by
324	    dsimp
325	    rw [comp_sum, sum_comp]
326	    refine Finset.sum_congr rfl fun x _ => ?_
327	    rw [comp_zsmul, zsmul_comp]
328	    congr 1
329	    symm
330	    apply f.naturality
331	
332	end AlternatingCofaceMapComplex
333	
334	variable (C)
335	
336	/-- The alternating coface map complex, as a functor -/
337	@[simps]
338	def alternatingCofaceMapComplex : CosimplicialObject C ⥤ CochainComplex C ℕ where
339	  obj := AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.obj
340	  map f := AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.map f

50	/-- The categorical product of a family of objects in `ModuleCat`
51	agrees with the usual module-theoretical product.

53	noncomputable def piIsoPi : ∏ᶜ Z ≅ ModuleCat.of R (∀ i, Z i) :=

59	    (piIsoPi Z).inv ≫ Pi.π Z i = ofHom (LinearMap.proj i : (∀ i : ι, Z i) →ₗ[R] Z i) :=

64	    (piIsoPi Z).hom ≫ ofHom (LinearMap.proj i : (∀ i : ι, Z i) →ₗ[R] Z i) = Pi.π Z i :=

   docstring of `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`. This is
   the heartbeats lift required for *any* further tactic work inside the
   `have h_diff_pi_smul_f` block: even adding two more tactic steps below
   `intro r y` previously cascaded into a `whnf` timeout at L412 (theorem
   head) because the `(r • _) j` smul forces `HSMul ↑R ↑(Z₂ j) ?_` synthesis
   that whnf-s through `h_mod_pi₂`'s `RingHom.toModule (presheaf.map …).hom`
   builder.

2. **Replaced `intro r y; sorry`** with the S1–prefix:
   ```
   intro r y
   funext j
   simp only [Pi.smul_apply]
   sorry
   ```
   `funext j` cleanly reduces to the per-output-index goal:
   ```
   e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = (r • e₂ ((scK₀.f).hom (e₁.symm y))) j
   ```
   `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` is a no-op as currently configured (the smul
   on the RHS is `h_mod_pi₂`'s, and simp can't unify it with `Pi.instSMul`
   without unfolding through `@Pi.module …`'s structure), but it's kept as
   intent documentation for the next iteration to layer over.

### What remains

The substantive content S2–S8 of the iter-073 recipe:

- **S2 (5-layer dsimp).** Unfold `scK₀.f` through
  `cechCochain → cechComplexFunctor → FormalCoproduct.cochainComplexFunctor →
   FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor + AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex`.
  After this, `(scK₀.f).hom` is exposed as `objD X (prev n)` where
  `X := (evalOp.obj P) ∘ ((mk … cech).rightOp)` for the underlying presheaf
  `P := (sheafToPresheaf _).obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
- **S3.** Use `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD` to expose
  the differential as `∑ k : Fin (prev n + 2), (-1)^k • X.δ k` where
  `X.δ k = X.map (op (SimplexCategory.δ k))`.
- **S4.** `Pi.smul_apply` on the LHS to evaluate `(r • y) (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom)`.
- **S5 (`Finset.smul_sum`).** Distribute `r •` across the alternating sum.
- **S6 (`Finset.sum_congr`).** Reduce to per-summand R-linearity.
- **S7 (per-summand).** For each `k`, the j-component of `X.δ k` (read off
  via `evalOp_obj_map` and `Pi.lift`) is
  `Pi.π Z₁ (j ∘ δ_k.toOrderHom) ≫ (P.map (φ_k j).op)` where `φ_k j` is a
  morphism in `Scheme.Opens` with `V_j ≤ V_{j ∘ δ_k}`. Per-component
  R-linearity then follows from:
  ```
  (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op).hom ((presheaf.map (V_{j∘δ_k} ≤ U).op).hom r * x)
    = (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ U).op).hom r * (presheaf.map (V_j ≤ V_{j∘δ_k}).op).hom x
  ```
  (ring-hom distributivity + presheaf-functoriality on the `(V_j ≤ U)` chain).
- **S8 (`rfl` or `ring`).** Close after the chain.

### Why this is hard

Three compounding obstacles:

1. **Heartbeat sensitivity.** Any rewrite touching the `(r • _) : Π → Π`
   smul triggers `HSMul ↑R ↑(Z_i j) ?_` synthesis that pushes whnf past the
   200000 default budget *at the theorem head*, not just locally. The
   `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in` lift this iteration is a necessary
   prerequisite for any further work.

2. **The `K₀.d ↔ objD` opacity.** `K₀.d (prev n) n` is `K₀.d` via
   `CochainComplex.of` + `(up ℕ).Rel (prev n) n` case-split. The "is-Rel"
   case is not reducible by `dsimp` without explicit
   `CochainComplex.of_d_eq_succ` rewrites for the `up ℕ` shape.

3. **Pi-product index gymnastics.** The differential's j-component
   identification requires chasing through `Pi.lift`/`Pi.π` + `evalOp.obj`
   on `FormalCoproduct.cosimplicialObjectFunctor.cech.rightOp`. The
   `evalOp_obj_map` simp lemma (verified in Mathlib at
   `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/Basic.lean` L383) provides
   the structural fact `(evalOp.obj F).map f = Pi.lift (fun i ↦ Pi.π _ (f.unop.f i) ≫ F.map (f.unop.φ i).op)`,
   but the chain through `cech.rightOp` and `powerMap` adds an extra layer.

### Approaches I tried and ruled out

- **`rw [Pi.smul_apply]`**: failed with "Did not find an occurrence of the
  pattern (?a • ?f) ?i". The smul instance on the RHS doesn't unify with
  `Pi.instSMul` without unfolding through `h_mod_pi₂`'s opaque builder.
- **`show e₂ (…) j = r • (e₂ (…)) j`**: failed with "failed to synthesize
  instance of type class HSMul ↑R ↑(Z₂ j) ?_". There's no standalone R-action
  on `Z₂ j` — only on the product, via `h_mod_pi₂`.
- **`exact (LinearEquiv.map_smul e₂ r _)`**: failed — `e₂` is `k`-linear, not
  `R`-linear.
- **Constructing `L : (∀ i, Z₁ i) →ₗ[R] (∀ i, Z₂ i)` directly**: doesn't avoid
  the work — `L.map_smul` still requires the substantive per-summand chain.

### Mathlib references confirmed this pass

- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_inv_kernel_ι` : `(piIsoPi Z).inv ≫ Pi.π Z i = ofHom (LinearMap.proj i)`
- `ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype` : `(piIsoPi Z).hom ≫ ofHom (LinearMap.proj i) = Pi.π Z i`
  (both `@[elementwise]` — gives applied forms)
- `CategoryTheory.Limits.FormalCoproduct.evalOp_obj_map` : the explicit
  `Pi.lift` form for the cosimplicial differential
- `AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD` :
  `objD X n = ∑ i : Fin (n + 2), (-1)^i • X.δ i`
- Project-local `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.algebraMap_naturality`
  (L161 of `StructureSheafModuleK.lean`) — the k-level version of per-component
  R-linearity. The R-level analogue is what S7 above needs.

## Other sorries in BasicOpenCech.lean (unchanged this lane)

- L496 (substep (a)): extra-degeneracy on `s`-indexed slice cover — dead end this lane.
- L820 (kernel acyclicity / `h_π_split` end-state): dead end this lane.
- L848 (substep (a) for `s₀`-indexed slice cover): dead end this lane.
- L1134 (`g_R.map_smul'`): downstream of `h_diff_pi_smul_f` — gated on Lane 2 landing.
- L1163 (`h_loc_exact`): needs `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` infrastructure — iter-079+.

## Recommendations for plan agent / next iteration

1. **Keep `set_option maxHeartbeats 800000 in`** — removing it will re-trigger
   the timeout cascade.
2. **Resume from the L1087–1094 state** — the `funext j` reduction is sound;
   build the S2 dsimp + S3 `objD` exposure on top.
3. **Consider `change`-based manual exposure** of the smul as a workaround
   for `simp only [Pi.smul_apply]` not firing — e.g., explicitly state the
   per-component smul formula via the per-i restriction map.
4. **Alternative**: skip the `funext`-then-rewrite approach entirely and
   construct `f_R` directly as `LinearMap.mk` using a `restrictScalars`-
   transported underlying differential — but this requires building the
   R-side cochain differential, which is itself iter-079+ work and depends
   on the same per-summand R-linearity content.

## Blueprint marker recommendation

No change. `h_diff_pi_smul_f` is internal to
`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf` and has no `\lean{...}`
hint of its own; the parent theorem already lacks `\leanok` (still has
sorries).

content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.
